Tests the subtraction of negative and positive large exponential numbers resulting in positive infinity.

# Purpose
This code is a simple test assertion that checks the behavior of JavaScript's arithmetic operations with very large numbers. It verifies that subtracting negative `1e+308` from positive `1e+308` results in positive infinity (`+Infinity`). This test ensures that the JavaScript engine correctly handles arithmetic operations involving numbers at the limits of floating-point precision. The code provides narrow functionality, focusing specifically on validating the correct handling of large number arithmetic.
